message:
perf(scope): replace Box::pin with stack pinning via std::pin::pin! macro

In Scope::race() and Scope::hedge(), join futures were heap-allocated
via Box::pin() on every call. Since these futures are only held locally
within the function scope and never escape, stack-pinning via the
std::pin::pin!() macro is sufficient and avoids allocation overhead in
what are often hot paths (race is used for speculative execution and
hedge for latency hedging).

Changes in race():
- Replace Box::pin(h1.join_with_drop_reason(...)) with pin!(f1) for
  both the winner and loser join futures
- Use pinned.as_mut() for Select::new() instead of &mut boxed
- Reuse the pinned loser future for drain await instead of creating
  a new h2.join(cx) / h1.join(cx) call, which was redundant since the
  JoinFuture was already constructed and partially polled

Changes in hedge():
- Same Box::pin → pin!() conversion for the primary join future,
  sleep future, and fallback join future
- Use as_mut() for Select::new() to satisfy Pin<&mut> requirements

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
